# emersonfelipesp.com

> Personal portfolio and open-source project showcase for Emerson Felipe — Software Developer and Network Automation Engineer based in Cotia, São Paulo, Brazil.

## About

Emerson Felipe is a software developer and network automation engineer passionate about Service Provider networks and open-source tooling. He works at N-Multifibra and contributes to the @netbox-community and @netdevopsbr communities.

- **Role**: Software Developer & Network Automation Engineer
- **Location**: Cotia, São Paulo, Brazil
- **Company**: N-Multifibra (@N-Multifibra)
- **Email**: emersonfelipe.2003@gmail.com
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/emersonfelipesp
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/emersonfelipesp/
- **Telegram**: https://t.me/emersonfelipesp

## Open-Source Projects

### netbox-proxbox
- **URL**: https://emersonfelipesp.com/netbox-proxbox
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/N-Multifibra/netbox-proxbox
- **Stars**: 539+
- **Description**: A NetBox plugin that synchronizes Proxmox virtual machine and container infrastructure into NetBox. Enables DCIM/IPAM teams to keep their NetBox source of truth automatically up to date with their Proxmox hypervisor inventory.
- **Stack**: Python, Django (NetBox plugin system), FastAPI (proxbox-api backend), Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time sync progress
- **Install**: `pip install netbox-proxbox`
- **Status**: Active, production-used at N-Multifibra

### netbox-sdk
- **URL**: https://emersonfelipesp.com/netbox-sdk
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/emersonfelipesp/netbox-sdk
- **Stars**: 14
- **Description**: A modern Python async SDK for interacting with the NetBox REST API, bundled with a CLI and a TUI (terminal user interface). Built with httpx, pydantic, and Typer. The TUI ships with 8 theme presets derived from popular editor themes (Dracula, Tokyo Night, One Dark Pro, Monokai, etc.).
- **Stack**: Python, httpx (async HTTP), Pydantic (data validation), Typer (CLI), Textual (TUI)
- **Install**: `pip install netbox-sdk`
- **Latest release**: v0.0.7.post6

### proxmox-sdk
- **URL**: https://emersonfelipesp.com/proxmox-sdk
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/emersonfelipesp/proxmox-sdk
- **Description**: A schema-driven Python SDK for the Proxmox REST API, auto-generated from the official Proxmox OpenAPI specification. Provides a FastAPI application layer on top of raw Proxmox API calls, with full type safety via Pydantic models.
- **Stack**: Python, FastAPI, Pydantic, OpenAPI/Swagger codegen
- **Install**: `pip install proxmox-sdk`
- **Status**: Alpha

## Technical Skills

- **Languages**: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, HTML, CSS
- **Frameworks**: Django, FastAPI, Next.js, Bootstrap, Jinja2
- **Databases**: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, SQLite
- **Platforms**: NetBox, Proxmox, Docker, Zabbix, Grafana, Linux
- **Network vendors**: Cisco, Huawei, Mikrotik, Dell, A10 Networks
- **Domains**: DCIM/IPAM, BGP, MPLS L2VPN, GPON, RPKI, Network Automation

## Site Architecture

This website is built with:
- **Framework**: Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack)
- **Language**: TypeScript 6
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first, no config file)
- **Hosting**: Vercel
- **Design aesthetic**: Terminal / CLI — monospace only, ASCII banners, box-drawing characters, prompt-style section headers

The site supports 10 color themes: `default-light`, `default-dark`, `netbox-dark`, `netbox-light`, `dracula`, `tokyo-night`, `onedark-pro`, `proxmox-dark`, `proxmox-light`, `monokai`. Theme is persisted in `localStorage`.

## Pages

- `/` — Profile, bio, featured projects grid, skills, contact form
- `/netbox-proxbox` — Full showcase for the netbox-proxbox plugin including screenshots, installation guide (3 paths), configuration steps, and repo stats
- `/netbox-sdk` — Showcase for the NetBox SDK with features, install instructions, and repo stats
- `/proxmox-sdk` — Showcase for the Proxmox SDK with features, install instructions, and repo stats
- `/sitemap.xml` — XML sitemap
- `/robots.txt` — Crawl directives
- `/llms.txt` — This file

## Contact

To reach Emerson: emersonfelipe.2003@gmail.com

The contact form on the homepage (`/`) accepts name, email, and message. Form submissions are validated server-side with Zod.
